stance: BULLISH — 6 BEATs + 2 HITs / 8 strict claims · AWS reaccel + AI capex absorbing margin
summary: Amazon delivered 6 strict BEATs and 2 HITs across 8 independent Q+1 net-sales guidance ranges — weighted MCS 0.875. Most recent 4 quarters all BEAT the HIGH end of guidance. Q4 2025 set a record at $213.4B (+13.6% YoY), and Q1 2026 came in at $181.5B (+16.6% YoY) — the fastest growth in 8 quarters. AWS reaccelerated to ~21% YoY in Q1 2026 with operating margin expanding to 13.1% (highest in the series). Massive AI capex acceleration ($44.2B in Q1 2026, +77% sequentially) is depressing near-term FCF but driving AWS capacity expansion. Stock $268.99 (May 11 close), RSI 72 (overbought near 52w high $278.56). FactSet target $313.55 = +16.6% upside. EV/EBITDA FY1 18.4x sits at -23% discount to mega-cap tech peer median.
quotes: AWS Q1 2026 revenue grew 21% year-over-year to $33.2B, with operating margin of 39.5%.
We expect Q2 2026 net sales of $182.5B to $187.5B and operating income of $19.5B to $24B.
We will spend approximately $125B in capital expenditures in 2026, the vast majority dedicated to AI infrastructure for AWS.
Q4 net sales of $213.4B exceeded the high end of our guidance range of $213B.
AWS grew 20.2% year-over-year to $33.1B in Q3 with an operating margin of 34.5%.
Our advertising business reached a $50B annualized run rate, growing 22% year-over-year.
bull: MCS 0.875 — 6 BEATs + 2 HITs across 8 strict claims
Six of eight Q+1 net-sales guides BEAT the HIGH end of the range. Two HITs sat at the upper boundary. Zero MISSes. Last 4 quarters (Q2'25 through Q1'26) all BEATs.

AWS revenue growth re-accelerated from 17.2% (Q2 2024) to 21% (Q1 2026). Q1 2026 AWS operating margin 39.5%. AI workloads driving capacity demand. Anthropic + foundation-model training are scale drivers.

Consolidated operating margin expanded from 9.9% (Q2 2024) to 13.1% (Q1 2026) — highest in the 8-quarter series. Retail (NA + International) operating margins both improved. International segment now profitable.
Capex run-rate accelerated from $25B/quarter (Q1 2025) to $44B/quarter (Q1 2026, +77% sequential). Management committed to ~$125B FY26 — supporting AWS capacity for hyperscaler-grade AI workloads.
Sponsored Ads + Prime Video ads + DSP now $50B+ run-rate growing ~22% YoY. Highest-margin segment after AWS.
EV/EBITDA FY1 18.4× sits -23% below large-cap tech peer median (24.0×). EV/Sales FY1 4.0× is -53% below peer median (8.5×) but reflects retail mix dilution. Sell-side target $313.55 = +16.6% upside.
bear: Q1 2026 FCF = -$18.2B (capex $44B vs OCF $26B). FCF margin in 8 quarters dropped from +12% in Q4 2024 to -10% in Q1 2026. Multi-year capex absorption window before AI-payback ROIs land.
$125B 2026 capex is 4x the FY23 run-rate. If AWS AI demand growth disappoints or hyperscaler price war erodes margins, multi-year ROIC dilution risk.
NA retail and international margins remain volatile QoQ. Macro consumer softness (tariff impact on cross-border, China sellers) creates revenue mix headwinds.
FTC monopolization case, EU DMA gatekeeper obligations, multi-state antitrust complaints. Potential structural remedy outcomes (Marketplace separation, AWS divestiture talk) overhang.
Stock near 52w high $278.56, RSI 72, MACD histogram negative (-0.41). Bollinger upper band approaching. Pullback risk in near term.
AWS share loss to Azure/GCP in AI
Azure AI revenue growing faster ($16B+ run-rate); Google Cloud Vertex AI gaining mindshare. AWS Bedrock + Trainium need to deliver on hyperscaler price-performance differentiation.
trigger_up: Q2 2026 ER (Jul 30, 2026): AWS reaccel above 22% YoY with operating margin >40%
FY2026 capex execution: signed AI customer wins to validate $125B spend
Operating margin sustains 13%+ for full year despite capex absorption
Trainium adoption / hyperscaler AI training wins announced
trigger_down: Q2 2026 ER misses or AWS decelerates below 18% YoY
FCF stays negative for full year 2026 (capex outpaces OCF)
Operating margin compresses below 11% on retail weakness
FTC monopolization case enters remedies phase
AI hyperscaler price war erodes AWS gross margins
bottom_line: Amazon is delivering an AI-era operating-margin expansion story while simultaneously digesting the largest capex acceleration in company history. The MCS is strong (6/8 BEATs, no MISSes) and the most recent 4 prints all hit the HIGH end. AWS reaccelerated to 21% YoY in Q1 2026 with 39.5% segment operating margin — the most important read. Trade-off: FCF goes negative in 2026 ($125B capex run-rate) but operating profits compound. Sell-side target $313.55 = +16.6% upside. Trade BULLISH but expect drawdowns when capex / FCF prints land — RSI 72 says wait for an entry pullback under $260.
disclaimer: Valuation snapshots are FactSet consensus captured 2026-05-11. Not investment advice.
